questions.question: Leptospirosis is a disease caused by ____:
questions.explanation: Leptospirosis is a bacterial disease caused by corkscrew-shaped bacteria called Leptospira. Signs and symptoms can range from none to mild such as headaches, muscle pains, and fevers; to severe with bleeding from the lungs or meningitis. If the infection causes the person to turn yellow, have kidney failure and bleeding, it is then known as Weil’s disease.

questions.question: Azolla increases soil fertility for ____:
questions.explanation: The nitrogen-fixing capability of Azolla an aquatic pteridophyte has led to it being widely used as a biofertiliser for increasing the productivity of rice fields. When rice paddies are flooded in the spring, they can be inoculated with Azolla, which then quickly multiplies to cover the water, suppressing weeds. The rotting plant material releases nitrogen to the rice plants, increasing the rice yield equivalent to that produced by 30-60 kg N/ha.

questions.question: Blood is a connective tissue embedded in which type of intercellular matrix?
questions.explanation: Blood is a fluid connective tissue. It has two components: cells and fluid matrix in which it is embedded. The liquid matrix is known as the plasma, in which blood cells are present. Blood travels all around the body in specialized blood vessels.

questions.question: The red, orange and yellow colours of leaf is due to ____:
questions.explanation: The red, orange and yellow colours of leaf are due to carotenoids contained in chromoplasts. When the green chlorophyll in leaves diminishes, the yellow pigments that already exist become dominant and give their color to the leaves.

questions.question: Which of the following amphibians lacks tongue?
questions.explanation: Ichthyophis lacks tongue. It is sometimes called the Asian caecilians. It is limbless amphibian. In Sri Lanka, three species occur. All are found in almost all habitats, but are known to prefer moist ones.

questions.question: Which one of the following is not the excretory organ?
questions.explanation: The excretory system is a collection of organs tasked with removing excess nitrogen and other toxins from the body. Several parts of the body such as sweat glands (excretion of sweat), liver (detoxifies and breaks down chemicals, poisons and other toxins that enter the body), lungs (disposal of carbon dioxide through exhalation) and kidneys (excretion of waste through urinary system) are involved in this process. The spleen also called grave yard of RBC is not considered as an excretory organ.

questions.question: The main use of salt in the diet is to ____:

options.label: A
options.text: Produce in small amounts the hydrochloric acid required for the digestion of food

options.label: B
options.text: Increase the solubility of the food particles in water

options.label: D
options.text: Make the taste of the food better

answer.label: A
answer.text: Produce in small amounts the hydrochloric acid required for the digestion of food

questions.explanation: Common table salt plays a primary role in the processes of digestion and absorption. Salt activates an enzyme in the mouth called salivary amylase. Salt also creates hydrochloric acid. Hydrochloric acid is a very important digestive secretion, which lines the stomach walls.

questions.question: The main function of palisade parenchyma in leaf is _____:
questions.explanation: Palisade parenchyma is the layer of tissue directly beneath the epidermis of the upper surface of a leaf. It contains the cylinder-shaped cells that hold the chloroplasts and constitutes the primary area of photosynthesis that converts the light energy to the chemical potential energy of carbohydrates.

questions.question: Name the tiny pores present on the surface of leaves in plants.
questions.explanation: Stomata are pores in the surface of the leaf. The pore is surrounded by two guard cells. When these two guard cells take up water, they bend and open up the pore between them.

questions.question: Which of the following full form of ATP is correct?
questions.explanation: ATP stands for Adenosine Triphosphate. It is a molecule that carries energy within cells. It is the main energy currency of the cell, and it is an end product of the processes of photophosphorylation (adding a phosphate group to a molecule using energy from light), cellular respiration, and fermentation.
